_README: Default PenDonn config. Empty MACs let interface_manager fall back to interface NAMES (less reliable across reboots — set monitor_mac/attack_mac/management_mac after running scripts/detect-wifi-adapters.sh). Empty secret_key triggers a per-startup random secret (sessions reset on restart) — set a persistent value via the installer or run web/app.py once to generate config.json.local. See config/config.example.json for an annotated reference.

wifi._comment: Interface names can swap on reboot — populate monitor_mac/attack_mac/management_mac for stable detection. management_interface MUST point to whichever iface carries SSH; safety preflight will refuse to start otherwise.

whitelist._comment: SSIDs in this list are NEVER targeted (no scanning, no deauth, no handshake capture, no enumeration). Add your home/work networks here BEFORE first run. Empty list = nothing protected.

web._comment: host defaults to 127.0.0.1 (loopback only). Change to 0.0.0.0 to expose on the LAN ONLY if you have authentication configured (basic_auth section, set in installer). Empty secret_key triggers ephemeral per-startup secret with a loud warning.

safety._comment: SSH-lockout protection. Defaults are intentionally strict. See docs/SAFETY.md before changing anything here.
